Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows hypoglycemia with low HbA1c, elevated creatinine, and hyperchloremia, suggesting possible renal impairment and metabolic disturbances. Urinalysis reveals proteinuria, ketonuria, bilirubinuria, low specific gravity, and abnormal urine appearance, indicating possible nephrotic syndrome or significant renal pathology. No evidence of urinary tract infection. Clinical correlation and further nephrological evaluation are recommended.
